prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, honey, imagine a tiny little factory inside your computer. This factory is called the Central Processing Unit, or CPU for short. It's like the boss of the computer, taking all the instructions you give it, like when you ask to open a game or watch a video, and turning them into actions the computer can understand. It's really quite clever, even if it is very, very small!
